switch.text: GetGrain(N) returns pointer into mapped ring

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 590px; height: 1px; padding-top: 923px; margin-left: 10px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0px; text-align: left;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: rgb(31, 41, 51); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; overflow-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Why ReadGrainNonBlocking + Go-side sleep
libfabric's util_wait.c returns -EINTR from epoll_wait as fatal 'poll failed'
in release builds (the EINTR filter is gated on #if ENABLE_DEBUG).
Go's async preemption sends SIGURG to running goroutines ~50/sec since Go 1.14;
the blocking ReadGrain receives that signal via the libfabric thread and tears
the endpoint down every 10–60s in steady state. Polling from Go avoids blocking
inside libfabric and sidesteps the signal-interaction.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 610px; height: 1px; padding-top: 923px; margin-left: 630px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0px; text-align: left;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: rgb(31, 41, 51); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; overflow-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Target fatal-error recovery
On any non-ErrNotReady error from ReadGrain, libmxl-fabrics may have left
dangling internal state - the next ReadGrain segfaults inside cgo. The
reconciler closes only the fabric triple (regions/target/info) and rebuilds it.
The mxl.Writer is KEPT so the on-disk flow definition and consumer FlowReader
handles stay valid. The new TargetInfo is published via status; the source side
picks up the rotation through its existing watch.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 510px; height: 1px; padding-top: 923px; margin-left: 1270px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0px; text-align: left;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: rgb(31, 41, 51); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; overflow-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Zero-copy commit on target
OpenGrain(N).Commit touches no payload bytes - the source initiator already
RDMA'd payload + header into the target's mapped ring slot. The Commit only
advances HeadIndex so consumer FlowReaders see the new grain.
